meta.source_paper: DeFi risk monitoring research, approval exploit post-mortems (Revoke.cash)

risk_domain.name: Behavioral Drift Monitoring
risk_domain.description: Long-term behavioral monitoring detecting gradual security and privacy degradation. Wallet + RPC guard that tracks portfolio concentration, leverage creep, approval accumulation, gas inefficiency, and behavioral fingerprinting over time.

capabilities: longitudinal on-chain behavioral analysis across all user addresses
approval monitoring to detect exploitable unlimited allowances
pattern recognition of recurring transaction sequences for fingerprinting
portfolio composition inference from token transfer patterns
limitations: cannot observe off-chain portfolio (CEX holdings, fiat)
behavioral drift detection requires extended observation period (weeks/months)

H1_portfolio_concentration.name: Portfolio concentration
H1_portfolio_concentration.description: More than 50% of on-chain portfolio value is concentrated in a single protocol, and this concentration is increasing over time. Creates correlated risk exposure and makes the user predictable to adversaries monitoring protocol-specific activity.

signals.name: single_protocol_dominance
signals.description: One protocol holds >50% of user's total on-chain value across all tracked addresses

signals.name: concentration_increasing
signals.description: Concentration in dominant protocol has increased by >10% over the past 30 days

signals.name: single_chain_concentration
signals.description: User's entire portfolio is on a single chain with no cross-chain diversification

recommendations.description: Alert user to concentration risk and suggest rebalancing across protocols
Configure maximum protocol concentration threshold that triggers warnings before new deposits

H1_portfolio_concentration.fundamental_limitation: Concentration may be intentional (yield farming, conviction bets). The system cannot distinguish deliberate strategy from inattentive drift without user context.

benchmark_scenario.setup: Track N wallets over 90 days, measure portfolio concentration changes
benchmark_scenario.metric: accuracy in detecting wallets that experienced protocol-specific losses due to concentration
benchmark_scenario.baseline: concentrated portfolios face 2-5x higher drawdowns in protocol-specific events

H2_leverage_creep.name: Leverage creep
H2_leverage_creep.description: User's total leverage across lending protocols is increasing week-over-week without corresponding collateral increases. Gradual leverage accumulation often goes unnoticed until a market downturn triggers cascading liquidations.

signals.name: leverage_increasing
signals.description: Total borrow/collateral ratio has increased for 3+ consecutive weeks

signals.name: multi_protocol_leverage
signals.description: User has open borrow positions across 2+ lending protocols simultaneously

signals.name: approaching_liquidation
signals.description: Aggregate health factor across all positions is below 1.3

recommendations.description: Show aggregate leverage across all protocols with historical trend and liquidation distances
Configure maximum aggregate leverage ratio that blocks new borrows when exceeded

H2_leverage_creep.fundamental_limitation: Leverage monitoring only covers on-chain positions. Users with CEX margin positions have hidden leverage that this system cannot detect, understating true risk.

benchmark_scenario.setup: Monitor N wallets with lending positions over 90 days through a market downturn
benchmark_scenario.metric: fraction of liquidated wallets that showed leverage creep signals beforehand
benchmark_scenario.baseline: most liquidated positions show 2-4 weeks of gradual leverage increase before event

H3_approval_accumulation.name: Approval accumulation
H3_approval_accumulation.description: Growing number of unlimited ERC-20 token approvals without periodic revocation. Each outstanding approval is an attack surface -- if an approved contract is exploited, all approved tokens can be drained.

signals.name: unlimited_approvals_count
signals.description: User has >10 outstanding unlimited (max uint256) token approvals

signals.name: stale_approvals
signals.description: Approvals granted to contracts the user hasn't interacted with in 90+ days

signals.name: approval_to_vulnerable_contract
signals.description: Outstanding approval to a contract with known vulnerability or audit finding

detection.threshold: approval_to_vulnerable_contract triggers ALERT, unlimited_approvals_count triggers WARNING

recommendations.description: Revoke approvals to contracts not interacted with in 90+ days
Set approval amounts to exact transaction needs instead of unlimited

H3_approval_accumulation.fundamental_limitation: ERC-20 approve() is permissive by design. Until permit2 or session-key patterns are universal, users must actively manage approvals. Gas costs of revocation deter hygiene.

benchmark_scenario.setup: Audit N wallets for outstanding approvals, cross-reference with exploit history
benchmark_scenario.metric: fraction of wallets with approvals to historically exploited contracts
benchmark_scenario.baseline: average active DeFi wallet has 20-50 outstanding unlimited approvals (Revoke.cash data)

H4_gas_spending_trend.name: Gas spending trend
H4_gas_spending_trend.description: User's gas costs are increasing over time, potentially indicating increasingly inefficient strategies, unnecessary complex transactions, or MEV extraction eroding effective returns.

signals.name: gas_cost_increasing
signals.description: Weekly gas spend has increased for 4+ consecutive weeks while transaction count is stable

signals.name: high_gas_ratio
signals.description: Gas costs exceed 5% of transaction value on average across recent transactions

signals.name: inefficient_routing
signals.description: Transactions use suboptimal gas patterns (high gas limit with low usage, manual gas price)

recommendations.description: Generate report of gas-inefficient transactions with suggestions for optimization
Suggest batching multiple operations into single transactions where possible

H4_gas_spending_trend.fundamental_limitation: Gas costs are partly a function of network conditions, not just user behavior. Rising base fees during congestion inflate gas costs without any change in user strategy.

benchmark_scenario.setup: Analyze gas spending patterns of N wallets over 90 days across varying network conditions
benchmark_scenario.metric: ability to distinguish user-caused gas inefficiency from network-caused increases
benchmark_scenario.baseline: power DeFi users spend 2-10% of portfolio value on gas annually

H5_interaction_pattern_rigidity.name: Interaction pattern rigidity
H5_interaction_pattern_rigidity.description: User follows the same sequence of on-chain actions at predictable times, creating a behavioral fingerprint. This pattern is both a privacy risk (linking addresses to the same entity) and a security risk (adversary can predict and front-run actions).

signals.name: temporal_regularity
signals.description: Transactions cluster at the same time of day across weeks with <1 hour variance

signals.name: sequence_repetition
signals.description: Same ordered sequence of contract interactions repeats 3+ times (e.g., swap -> deposit -> borrow)

signals.name: day_of_week_pattern
signals.description: User consistently transacts on specific days of the week (e.g., always Monday + Thursday)

recommendations.description: Add random delays (1-6 hours) to recurring transactions to break temporal patterns
Occasionally reorder non-dependent operations to break sequence fingerprints

H5_interaction_pattern_rigidity.fundamental_limitation: Some patterns are operationally necessary (e.g., weekly yield harvesting). Users cannot fully randomize behavior that is constrained by protocol mechanics or personal schedules.

benchmark_scenario.setup: Analyze N wallets over 90 days, identify recurring patterns, test linkability
benchmark_scenario.metric: adversary accuracy in linking multiple addresses to same entity via behavioral patterns
benchmark_scenario.baseline: behavioral fingerprinting can narrow anonymity set by 80-90% for habitual users

portfolio_tracker.description: Track portfolio composition across protocols and chains, detect concentration trends

leverage_monitor.description: Calculate total leverage across all lending positions with liquidation distance estimates

approval_auditor.description: Scan all outstanding token approvals, identify stale and unlimited approvals, suggest revocations

combined_benchmark.description: End-to-end benchmark measuring behavioral drift detection accuracy over extended monitoring periods

methodology.step_1: Select N wallets with 90+ days of DeFi activity history
methodology.step_2: Run all 5 heuristics on historical data with sliding window analysis
methodology.step_3: Correlate detected drift signals with actual adverse outcomes (liquidations, exploits, gas waste)
methodology.step_4: Measure detection lead time: how far in advance drift signals predicted the adverse event
methodology.step_5: Calculate false positive rate on wallets that experienced no adverse outcomes

combined_benchmark.target: detect 70% of adverse outcomes with 2+ week lead time and <20% false positive rate

adversary_levels.name: passive_monitor
adversary_levels.description: Portfolio and gas trend analysis only

adversary_levels.name: full_behavioral
adversary_levels.description: All behavioral drift heuristics including fingerprinting

templates.risk_assessment: Behavioral drift detected: {heuristic_id} ({heuristic_name}): {signal_description}. Confidence: {confidence}. Trend: {trend_direction} over {time_window}. Recommendation: {recommendation}.
templates.summary: Behavioral analysis over {time_window}: {n_signals} drift signals detected. {n_critical} critical. Portfolio concentration: {concentration_pct}%. Leverage trend: {leverage_trend}. Outstanding approvals: {approval_count}.
templates.skill_suggestion: To address {heuristic_name}, I can {skill_action}. This reduces drift risk by ~{effectiveness_pct}%. {user_cost_note}
